title: Client Onboarding Failure Rate
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:clienthealth`. from the `clienthealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~3 events per poll (ALL, WIRED, WIRELESS categories). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `scoreCategory` (string), `value` (integer 0-100), `clientCount` (integer), `scoreDetail{}` (array/object).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth" | spath output=categories path=scoreDetail{} | mvexpand categories | spath input=categories | where scoreCategory="ONBOARDING" | stats latest(value) as onboarding_score latest(clientCount) as affected_clients by _time | where onboarding_score < 50 | sort -affected_clients
description: Monitors the percentage of clients failing to onboard successfully (DHCP, AAA, or association failures), detecting authentication and connectivity infrastructure problems.
value: Onboarding failures prevent users from connecting at all. High failure rates indicate RADIUS server issues, DHCP pool exhaustion, or AP capacity problems.
implementation: Requires UC-5.13.9. The ONBOARDING branch must be present in `scoreDetail`; if not, verify Catalyst Center release and that client health is licensed for the sites you need. Tighten the threshold from 50 if your SLOs demand stricter onboarding scores; alert with P2 routing when `affected_clients` grows.
visualization: Table of affected waves in time, single value of worst `onboarding_score`, timechart of rolling minimum onboarding score for early warning.

references.title: Catalyst Center Client Health API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Scheduled RADIUS server maintenance window causing temporary onboarding failures.** During RADIUS/ISE maintenance, 802.1X authentication fails for all clients attempting to onboard, creating a spike in onboarding failures. Distinguish by correlating with ISE maintenance schedules or `index=ise sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` for authentication service unavailability. Suppress by using a `radius_maintenance_windows` lookup and filtering onboarding failures during those windows.

**Mass endpoint reboot (e.g., Windows Update, lab power cycle) creating a simultaneous onboarding surge.** A coordinated reboot of many endpoints causes a burst of onboarding attempts that may exceed the infrastructure's capacity, temporarily elevating the failure rate. Distinguish by checking whether the onboarding failure count and total onboarding attempt count both spike simultaneously. Suppress by normalizing the failure rate as a percentage of total attempts rather than using an absolute count threshold.

**New 802.1X policy deployment causing temporarily elevated failure rates.** When a new authentication policy is pushed to ISE or the network, some clients may fail to onboard until their supplicants update. Distinguish by correlating with `index=ise sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` for policy change events and checking which client types are failing. This is a real finding — do not suppress, but enrich the alert with the policy change context.

**Guest portal captive portal timeout appearing as onboarding failure.** Clients connecting to a guest SSID that requires captive portal authentication may time out during the portal interaction, appearing as onboarding failures in the Assurance data. Distinguish by checking whether the failures are concentrated on guest SSIDs. Suppress by separating guest and corporate onboarding metrics in the dashboard.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) ≥1.0 with the **clienthealth** modular input enabled, writing to `index=catalyst` with `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:clienthealth`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with Assurance licensing for client onboarding detail.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.
• Understanding of acceptable onboarding failure rates for your environment — typical enterprise targets: <5% overall, <2% for corporate SSIDs.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-health` at the configured interval (default 900 seconds). Client health data includes onboarding success/failure metrics aggregated by Catalyst Center's Assurance engine.

Key fields for onboarding failure rate:
• `scoreDetail` or `onboardingScore`: contains the onboarding success rate as a percentage or score.
• `totalOnboardingAttempts`: total onboarding attempts in the reporting period.
• `failedOnboardingAttempts` or similar: count of failed attempts.
• `onboardingFailureCategory`: category of failure (ASSOC, AUTH, DHCP, AAA).
• `band`: wireless band (2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, 6 GHz) — failure rates may differ by band.
• `ssid`: SSID where failures occurred.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth"
| spath scoreDetail{}.scoreCategory
| spath scoreDetail{}.scoreValue
| where 'scoreDetail{}.scoreCategory'="ONBOARDING"
| stats latest(scoreDetail{}.scoreValue) as onboarding_score by siteId
| eval failure_rate=100-onboarding_score
| eval status=case(failure_rate > 20, "Critical", failure_rate > 10, "High", failure_rate > 5, "Elevated", 1==1, "Normal")
| where status!="Normal"
| sort -failure_rate
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`spath scoreDetail{}.scoreCategory`**: Extracts the nested onboarding score from the JSON structure. Catalyst Center client health data uses nested JSON arrays for score details.
• **`where scoreCategory="ONBOARDING"`**: Filters to the onboarding score specifically — other categories include CONNECTIVITY and APPLICATION.
• **`failure_rate=100-onboarding_score`**: Converts the success score to a failure rate for alerting. A 95% onboarding score = 5% failure rate.
• **`status=case(...)`**: Classifies the failure rate into severity levels based on operational thresholds.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health > Client Health. Check the "Onboarding" health score. Compare with the Splunk results for the same time window and site.
• **Per-site check:** Select specific sites and compare onboarding scores between Catalyst Center and Splunk.
• **Failure category breakdown:** In Catalyst Center, drill into the onboarding failures to see ASSOC/AUTH/DHCP breakdowns. Compare with the Splunk category analysis.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Single-value tile for overall onboarding score, per-site onboarding failure rate table, and a timechart showing onboarding failure rate trends over 7 days.
• **Alert:** Trigger when `failure_rate > 10` for any site with >100 onboarding attempts. Throttle per `siteId` for 1 hour to avoid alert fatigue.
• **Root cause workflow:** High AUTH failure rate → check RADIUS/ISE. High DHCP failure rate → check DHCP server capacity and scope. High ASSOC failure rate → check AP capacity and RF coverage.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Onboarding score always shows 0 or null:** The `scoreDetail` JSON structure may differ across Catalyst Center versions. Run `| spath` on the raw events to identify the correct path to the onboarding score.
• **MDM re-enrollment inflating failure rates:** MDM-managed devices periodically re-enroll, generating expected authentication retries. These are typically transient and self-resolving.
• **Guest portal failures dominating:** Guest captive portal authentication flows generate expected failures during the redirect process. Filter guest SSIDs from the corporate onboarding failure rate analysis.
• If data is not arriving for `cisco:dnac:clienthealth`, check that the `clienthealth` input is enabled in the TA configuration and that the Catalyst Center API credentials have not expired.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how many people fail to connect to the network in the first place, catching problems with login servers, IP addresses, or overloaded access points.
